title: Dream2Flow: Bridging Video Generation and Open-World Manipulation with 3D Object Flow
summary: Generative video modeling has emerged as a compelling tool to zero-shot reason about plausible physical interactions for open-world manipulation. Yet, it remains a challenge to translate such human-led motions into the low-level actions demanded by robotic systems. We observe that given an initial image and task instruction, these models excel at synthesizing sensible object motions. Thus, we introduce Dream2Flow, a framework that bridges video generation and robotic control through 3D object flow as an intermediate representation. Our method reconstructs 3D object motions from generated videos and formulates manipulation as object trajectory tracking. By separating the state changes from the actuators that realize those changes, Dream2Flow overcomes the embodiment gap and enables zero-shot guidance from pre-trained video models to manipulate objects of diverse categories-including rigid, articulated, deformable, and granular. Through trajectory optimization or reinforcement learning, Dream2Flow converts reconstructed 3D object flow into executable low-level commands without task-specific demonstrations. Simulation and real-world experiments highlight 3D object flow as a general and scalable interface for adapting video generation models to open-world robotic manipulation. Videos and visualizations are available at https://dream2flow.github.io/.
tldr: Dream2Flow uses 3D object flow reconstructed from generated videos as an intermediate representation to bridge video generation and robotic control for zero-shot open-world manipulation.
tldr_zh: Dream2Flow 使用从生成视频中重建的 3D 对象流作为中间表示，以桥接视频生成和机器人控制，从而实现零样本开放世界操作。
summary_zh: 生成式视频建模已成为一种引人注目的工具，可用于对开放世界操控中合理的物理交互进行零样本推理。然而，如何将这种人为引导的运动转化为机器人系统所需的底层动作仍然是一个挑战。我们观察到，给定初始图像和任务指令，这些模型擅长合成合理的物体运动。因此，我们提出了 Dream2Flow，一个通过将 3D 对象流作为中间表示来桥接视频生成和机器人控制的框架。我们的方法从生成的视频中重建 3D 对象运动，并将操控过程形式化为物体轨迹跟踪。通过将状态改变与实现这些改变的执行器分离，Dream2Flow 克服了具身差距，并实现了从预训练视频模型到操控各类物体（包括刚性、铰接、可变形和颗粒状物体）的零样本指导。通过轨迹优化或强化学习，Dream2Flow 将重建的 3D 对象流转换为可执行的底层命令，而无需特定于任务的演示。仿真和真实世界实验突出了 3D 对象流作为通用且可扩展的接口，用于将视频生成模型适配于开放世界机器人操控。视频和可视化效果可在 https://dream2flow.github.io/ 获得。

title: LSRE: Latent Semantic Rule Encoding for Real-Time Semantic Risk Detection in Autonomous Driving
summary: Real-world autonomous driving must adhere to complex human social rules that extend beyond legally codified traffic regulations. Many of these semantic constraints, such as yielding to emergency vehicles, complying with traffic officers' gestures, or stopping for school buses, are intuitive for humans yet difficult to encode explicitly. Although large vision-language models (VLMs) can interpret such semantics, their inference cost makes them impractical for real-time deployment.This work proposes LSRE, a Latent Semantic Rule Encoding framework that converts sparsely sampled VLM judgments into decision boundaries within the latent space of a recurrent world model. By encoding language-defined safety semantics into a lightweight latent classifier, LSRE enables real-time semantic risk assessment at 10 Hz without per-frame VLM queries. Experiments on six semantic-failure scenarios in CARLA demonstrate that LSRE attains semantic risk detection accuracy comparable to a large VLM baseline, while providing substantially earlier hazard anticipation and maintaining low computational latency. LSRE further generalizes to rarely seen semantic-similar test cases, indicating that language-guided latent classification offers an effective and deployable mechanism for semantic safety monitoring in autonomous driving.
tldr: The paper proposes LSRE, a framework that converts VLM judgments into a lightweight latent classifier within a recurrent world model, achieving real-time semantic risk assessment in autonomous driving comparable to VLMs but with significantly lower latency.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了LSRE，一个将VLM判断转化为循环世界模型中轻量级潜在分类器的框架，实现了与VLM相当的自动驾驶实时语义风险评估，但延迟大大降低。
summary_zh: 真实世界的自动驾驶必须遵守复杂的、超越法律规定的交通规则的人类社会规则。许多这样的语义约束，例如避让紧急车辆、服从交通警察的手势或为校车停车，对人类来说是直观的，但很难明确编码。尽管大型视觉-语言模型(VLMs)可以解释这些语义，但它们的推理成本使得它们不适合实时部署。这项工作提出了LSRE，一种潜在语义规则编码框架，该框架将稀疏采样的VLM判断转换为循环世界模型潜在空间中的决策边界。通过将语言定义的安全性语义编码到轻量级的潜在分类器中，LSRE能够以10赫兹的实时速率进行语义风险评估，而无需逐帧VLM查询。在CARLA中对六个语义失效场景的实验表明，LSRE获得了与大型VLM基线相当的语义风险检测精度，同时提供了更早的危险预判并保持了较低的计算延迟。LSRE进一步推广到罕见的语义相似测试用例，表明语言引导的潜在分类为自动驾驶中的语义安全监控提供了一种有效且可部署的机制。

title: RoboMIND 2.0: A Multimodal, Bimanual Mobile Manipulation Dataset for Generalizable Embodied Intelligence
summary: While data-driven imitation learning has revolutionized robotic manipulation, current approaches remain constrained by the scarcity of large-scale, diverse real-world demonstrations. Consequently, the ability of existing models to generalize across long-horizon bimanual tasks and mobile manipulation in unstructured environments remains limited. To bridge this gap, we present RoboMIND 2.0, a comprehensive real-world dataset comprising over 310K dual-arm manipulation trajectories collected across six distinct robot embodiments and 739 complex tasks. Crucially, to support research in contact-rich and spatially extended tasks, the dataset incorporates 12K tactile-enhanced episodes and 20K mobile manipulation trajectories. Complementing this physical data, we construct high-fidelity digital twins of our real-world environments, releasing an additional 20K-trajectory simulated dataset to facilitate robust sim-to-real transfer. To fully exploit the potential of RoboMIND 2.0, we propose MIND-2 system, a hierarchical dual-system frame-work optimized via offline reinforcement learning. MIND-2 integrates a high-level semantic planner (MIND-2-VLM) to decompose abstract natural language instructions into grounded subgoals, coupled with a low-level Vision-Language-Action executor (MIND-2-VLA), which generates precise, proprioception-aware motor actions.
tldr: The paper introduces RoboMIND 2.0, a large-scale multimodal dataset for bimanual mobile manipulation, including real-world and simulated data, along with the MIND-2 system, a hierarchical framework for instruction-following using offline reinforcement learning.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了RoboMIND 2.0，一个用于双臂移动操作的大规模多模态数据集，包括真实世界和模拟数据，以及MIND-2系统，一个使用离线强化学习的用于指令跟随的分层框架。
summary_zh: 数据驱动的模仿学习极大地推动了机器人操作技术的发展，但当前的方法仍然受限于大规模、多样化真实世界演示数据的稀缺性。因此，现有模型在非结构化环境中推广到长时程双臂任务和移动操作的能力依然有限。为了弥补这一差距，我们提出了 RoboMIND 2.0，这是一个全面的真实世界数据集，包含在六种不同的机器人形态和 739 个复杂任务中收集的超过 31 万条双臂操作轨迹。至关重要的是，为了支持对接触密集型和空间扩展型任务的研究，该数据集纳入了 1.2 万条触觉增强型episode和 2 万条移动操作轨迹。为了补充这些物理数据，我们构建了真实世界环境的高保真数字孪生体，并额外发布了 2 万条轨迹的模拟数据集，以促进稳健的虚实迁移。为了充分发挥 RoboMIND 2.0 的潜力，我们提出了 MIND-2 系统，这是一种通过离线强化学习优化的分层双系统框架。MIND-2 集成了一个高层语义规划器（MIND-2-VLM）以将抽象的自然语言指令分解为具象的子目标，以及一个低层视觉-语言-动作执行器（MIND-2-VLA），该执行器生成精确的、本体感觉感知的运动指令。

title: Counterfactual VLA: Self-Reflective Vision-Language-Action Model with Adaptive Reasoning
summary: Recent reasoning-augmented Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have improved the interpretability of end-to-end autonomous driving by generating intermediate reasoning traces. Yet these models primarily describe what they perceive and intend to do, rarely questioning whether their planned actions are safe or appropriate. This work introduces Counterfactual VLA (CF-VLA), a self-reflective VLA framework that enables the model to reason about and revise its planned actions before execution. CF-VLA first generates time-segmented meta-actions that summarize driving intent, and then performs counterfactual reasoning conditioned on both the meta-actions and the visual context. This step simulates potential outcomes, identifies unsafe behaviors, and outputs corrected meta-actions that guide the final trajectory generation. To efficiently obtain such self-reflective capabilities, we propose a rollout-filter-label pipeline that mines high-value scenes from a base (non-counterfactual) VLA's rollouts and labels counterfactual reasoning traces for subsequent training rounds. Experiments on large-scale driving datasets show that CF-VLA improves trajectory accuracy by up to 17.6%, enhances safety metrics by 20.5%, and exhibits adaptive thinking: it only enables counterfactual reasoning in challenging scenarios. By transforming reasoning traces from one-shot descriptions to causal self-correction signals, CF-VLA takes a step toward self-reflective autonomous driving agents that learn to think before they act.
tldr: The paper introduces Counterfactual VLA (CF-VLA), a self-reflective Vision-Language-Action model for autonomous driving that uses counterfactual reasoning to identify and correct unsafe planned actions, leading to improved trajectory accuracy and safety.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了Counterfactual VLA (CF-VLA)，一种用于自动驾驶的自反思视觉-语言-动作模型，它使用反事实推理来识别和纠正不安全的计划动作，从而提高轨迹准确性和安全性。
summary_zh: 最近增强推理的视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型通过生成中间推理轨迹提高了端到端自动驾驶的可解释性。然而，这些模型主要描述它们感知到的内容和打算做的事情，很少质疑它们计划的行动是否安全或适当。本文介绍反事实VLA（CF-VLA），一种自反思的VLA框架，使模型能够在执行之前推理和修改其计划的行动。CF-VLA首先生成时间分段的元动作，概括驾驶意图，然后基于元动作和视觉上下文执行反事实推理。此步骤模拟潜在的结果，识别不安全的行为，并输出校正后的元动作，以指导最终的轨迹生成。为了有效地获得这种自我反思能力，我们提出了一个滚动-过滤-标记流程，该流程从基础（非反事实）VLA的滚动中挖掘高价值场景，并为后续训练轮次标记反事实推理轨迹。在大型驾驶数据集上的实验表明，CF-VLA将轨迹精度提高了高达17.6%，将安全指标提高了20.5%，并表现出适应性思维：它仅在具有挑战性的场景中启用反事实推理。通过将推理轨迹从一次性描述转化为因果性的自我纠正信号，CF-VLA朝着自反思的自动驾驶智能体迈出了一步，这些智能体学会了三思而后行。

title: Iterative Deployment Improves Planning Skills in LLMs
summary: We show that iterative deployment of large language models (LLMs), each fine-tuned on data carefully curated by users from the previous models' deployment, can significantly change the properties of the resultant models. By testing this mechanism on various planning domains, we observe substantial improvements in planning skills, with later models displaying emergent generalization by discovering much longer plans than the initial models. We then provide theoretical analysis showing that iterative deployment effectively implements reinforcement learning (RL) training in the outer-loop (i.e. not as part of intentional model training), with an implicit reward function. The connection to RL has two important implications: first, for the field of AI safety, as the reward function entailed by repeated deployment is not defined explicitly, and could have unexpected implications to the properties of future model deployments. Second, the mechanism highlighted here can be viewed as an alternative training regime to explicit RL, relying on data curation rather than explicit rewards.
tldr: The paper demonstrates that iteratively deploying LLMs and fine-tuning them on user-curated data from previous deployments significantly improves planning skills and leads to emergent generalization, effectively implementing reinforcement learning in an outer loop.
tldr_zh: 该论文展示了迭代部署大型语言模型并在用户整理的先前部署数据上对其进行微调，可以显著提高规划技能并导致涌现式泛化，从而有效地在外部循环中实现强化学习。
summary_zh: 我们证明了大型语言模型（LLMs）的迭代部署，每次都利用用户精心挑选的，基于先前模型部署生成的数据进行微调，能够显著改变最终模型的特性。通过在多个规划领域测试这种机制，我们观察到规划技能的显著提升，后续模型通过发现比初始模型更长的计划，展现出涌现的泛化能力。然后，我们提供了理论分析，表明迭代部署有效地实现了外循环中的强化学习（RL）训练（即，不是作为有意模型训练的一部分），并带有一个隐式的奖励函数。与RL的这种联系具有两个重要的意义：首先，对于人工智能安全领域，由于重复部署所蕴含的奖励函数并未明确定义，可能对未来模型部署的特性产生意想不到的影响。其次，本文强调的这种机制可以被视为一种替代显式RL的训练方式，依赖于数据策展而非显式奖励。

title: MCPAgentBench: A Real-world Task Benchmark for Evaluating LLM Agent MCP Tool Use
summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly serving as autonomous agents, and their utilization of external tools via the Model Context Protocol (MCP) is considered a future trend. Current MCP evaluation sets suffer from issues such as reliance on external MCP services and a lack of difficulty awareness. To address these limitations, we propose MCPAgentBench, a benchmark based on real-world MCP definitions designed to evaluate the tool-use capabilities of agents. We construct a dataset containing authentic tasks and simulated MCP tools. The evaluation employs a dynamic sandbox environment that presents agents with candidate tool lists containing distractors, thereby testing their tool selection and discrimination abilities. Furthermore, we introduce comprehensive metrics to measure both task completion rates and execution efficiency. Experiments conducted on various latest mainstream Large Language Models reveal significant performance differences in handling complex, multi-step tool invocations. All code is open-source at Github.
tldr: MCPAgentBench, a new benchmark with real-world MCP definitions and a dynamic sandbox environment, is proposed to evaluate the tool-use capabilities of LLM agents, showing performance differences in complex tool invocations.
tldr_zh: MCPAgentBench是一个新的基准测试，它具有真实的MCP定义和一个动态沙盒环境，用于评估LLM代理的工具使用能力，并显示了复杂工具调用中的性能差异。
summary_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）正日益被用作自治代理，并通过模型上下文协议（MCP）利用外部工具被认为是未来的趋势。当前的MCP评估集存在对外部MCP服务的依赖以及缺乏难度感知等问题。为了解决这些局限性，我们提出了MCPAgentBench，这是一个基于真实MCP定义的基准，旨在评估代理的工具使用能力。我们构建了一个包含真实任务和模拟MCP工具的数据集。评估采用动态沙盒环境，向代理呈现包含干扰项的候选工具列表，从而测试其工具选择和辨别能力。此外，我们引入了全面的指标来衡量任务完成率和执行效率。对各种最新的主流大型语言模型进行的实验揭示了它们在处理复杂的多步骤工具调用方面的显著性能差异。所有代码均已在Github上开源。

title: DarkEQA: Benchmarking Vision-Language Models for Embodied Question Answering in Low-Light Indoor Environments
summary: Vision Language Models (VLMs) are increasingly adopted as central reasoning modules for embodied agents. Existing benchmarks evaluate their capabilities under ideal, well-lit conditions, yet robust 24/7 operation demands performance under a wide range of visual degradations, including low-light conditions at night or in dark environments--a core necessity that has been largely overlooked. To address this underexplored challenge, we present DarkEQA, an open-source benchmark for evaluating EQA-relevant perceptual primitives under multi-level low-light conditions. DarkEQA isolates the perception bottleneck by evaluating question answering from egocentric observations under controlled degradations, enabling attributable robustness analysis. A key design feature of DarkEQA is its physical fidelity: visual degradations are modeled in linear RAW space, simulating physics-based illumination drop and sensor noise followed by an ISP-inspired rendering pipeline. We demonstrate the utility of DarkEQA by evaluating a wide range of state-of-the-art VLMs and Low-Light Image Enhancement (LLIE) models. Our analysis systematically reveals VLMs' limitations when operating under these challenging visual conditions. Our code and benchmark dataset will be released upon acceptance.
tldr: This paper introduces DarkEQA, a new benchmark for evaluating Vision-Language Models in low-light embodied question answering, highlighting the robustness limitations of current VLMs in such conditions.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了 DarkEQA，一个新的基准，用于评估视觉-语言模型在弱光具身问答中的表现，强调了当前 VLM 在此类条件下鲁棒性的局限性。
summary_zh: 视觉语言模型（VLMs）正日益被采用为具身智能体的核心推理模块。现有的基准测试在理想、光照良好的条件下评估它们的能力，然而，稳健的24/7运行需要在各种视觉退化条件下都能表现出色，包括夜间或黑暗环境中的低光照条件——这一核心需求在很大程度上被忽视了。为了应对这一未被充分探索的挑战，我们提出了DarkEQA，一个开源基准测试，用于评估多层次低光照条件下相关的实体问答（EQA）感知原语。DarkEQA通过在受控退化条件下，从自我中心视角观察进行问答评估，从而隔离了感知瓶颈，实现了可归因的鲁棒性分析。DarkEQA的一个关键设计特点是其物理保真度：视觉退化在线性RAW空间中建模，模拟了基于物理的光照下降和传感器噪声，随后是一个受ISP启发的渲染管线。我们通过评估各种最先进的VLMs和低光照图像增强（LLIE）模型，证明了DarkEQA的效用。我们的分析系统地揭示了VLMs在这些具有挑战性的视觉条件下运行时的局限性。我们的代码和基准数据集将在通过审核后发布。

title: ArtiSG: Functional 3D Scene Graph Construction via Human-demonstrated Articulated Objects Manipulation
summary: 3D scene graphs have empowered robots with semantic understanding for navigation and planning, yet they often lack the functional information required for physical manipulation, particularly regarding articulated objects. Existing approaches for inferring articulation mechanisms from static observations are prone to visual ambiguity, while methods that estimate parameters from state changes typically rely on constrained settings such as fixed cameras and unobstructed views. Furthermore, fine-grained functional elements like small handles are frequently missed by general object detectors. To bridge this gap, we present ArtiSG, a framework that constructs functional 3D scene graphs by encoding human demonstrations into structured robotic memory. Our approach leverages a robust articulation data collection pipeline utilizing a portable setup to accurately estimate 6-DoF articulation trajectories and axes even under camera ego-motion. We integrate these kinematic priors into a hierarchical and open-vocabulary graph while utilizing interaction data to discover inconspicuous functional elements missed by visual perception. Extensive real-world experiments demonstrate that ArtiSG significantly outperforms baselines in functional element recall and articulation estimation precision. Moreover, we show that the constructed graph serves as a reliable functional memory that effectively guides robots to perform language-directed manipulation tasks in real-world environments containing diverse articulated objects.
tldr: The paper introduces ArtiSG, a framework that constructs functional 3D scene graphs from human demonstrations to enable robots to better manipulate articulated objects by encoding articulation trajectories and discovering functional elements.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了 ArtiSG，一个通过人类演示构建功能性 3D 场景图的框架，通过编码关节运动轨迹和发现功能元件，使机器人能够更好地操作铰接物体。
summary_zh: 三维场景图赋予了机器人语义理解能力，从而进行导航和规划，但它们通常缺乏物理操作所需的功能信息，尤其是在铰接对象方面。现有的从静态观测中推断铰接机制的方法容易产生视觉歧义，而从状态变化估计参数的方法通常依赖于受限的设置，如固定摄像头和无遮挡的视野。此外，通用目标检测器经常遗漏诸如小把手等细粒度的功能元素。为了弥合这一差距，我们提出了ArtiSG，一个通过将人类演示编码到结构化机器人记忆中来构建功能性三维场景图的框架。我们的方法利用一个鲁棒的铰接数据收集流程，该流程采用便携式设置，即使在摄像头发生自我运动的情况下也能准确估计六自由度的铰接轨迹和轴线。我们将这些运动学先验信息整合到一个分层且开放词汇的图中，同时利用交互数据来发现视觉感知遗漏的不起眼的功能元素。大量的真实世界实验表明，ArtiSG在功能元素召回率和铰接估计精度方面显著优于基线方法。 此外，我们还展示了所构建的图可以作为一种可靠的功能记忆，有效地指导机器人在包含各种铰接对象的真实世界环境中执行语言引导的操纵任务。

title: Dynamic Policy Learning for Legged Robot with Simplified Model Pretraining and Model Homotopy Transfer
summary: Generating dynamic motions for legged robots remains a challenging problem. While reinforcement learning has achieved notable success in various legged locomotion tasks, producing highly dynamic behaviors often requires extensive reward tuning or high-quality demonstrations. Leveraging reduced-order models can help mitigate these challenges. However, the model discrepancy poses a significant challenge when transferring policies to full-body dynamics environments. In this work, we introduce a continuation-based learning framework that combines simplified model pretraining and model homotopy transfer to efficiently generate and refine complex dynamic behaviors. First, we pretrain the policy using a single rigid body model to capture core motion patterns in a simplified environment. Next, we employ a continuation strategy to progressively transfer the policy to the full-body environment, minimizing performance loss. To define the continuation path, we introduce a model homotopy from the single rigid body model to the full-body model by gradually redistributing mass and inertia between the trunk and legs. The proposed method not only achieves faster convergence but also demonstrates superior stability during the transfer process compared to baseline methods. Our framework is validated on a range of dynamic tasks, including flips and wall-assisted maneuvers, and is successfully deployed on a real quadrupedal robot.
tldr: This paper presents a continuation-based reinforcement learning framework that combines simplified model pretraining with model homotopy transfer to efficiently train dynamic locomotion skills on legged robots and demonstrates its effectiveness on real-world tasks.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种基于连续的强化学习框架，该框架结合了简化的模型预训练和模型同伦转移，以有效地训练腿式机器人的动态运动技能，并在实际任务中展示了其有效性。
summary_zh: 为腿式机器人生成动态运动仍然是一个具有挑战性的问题。虽然强化学习在各种腿式运动任务中取得了显著的成功，但产生高度动态的行为通常需要大量的奖励调整或高质量的示范。利用降阶模型可以帮助缓解这些挑战。然而，模型差异在将策略转移到全身动力学环境中时会带来重大挑战。在这项工作中，我们介绍了一种基于连续性的学习框架，该框架结合了简化的模型预训练和模型同伦转移，以有效地生成和改进复杂的动态行为。首先，我们使用单刚体模型预训练策略，以在简化的环境中捕获核心运动模式。接下来，我们采用连续性策略逐步将策略转移到全身环境中，从而最大限度地减少性能损失。为了定义连续路径，我们通过逐步重新分配躯干和腿之间的质量和惯性，引入了从单刚体模型到全身模型的模型同伦。与基线方法相比，所提出的方法不仅实现了更快的收敛速度，而且在转移过程中表现出卓越的稳定性。我们的框架在一系列动态任务（包括翻转和墙壁辅助动作）上得到了验证，并已成功部署在真实的四足机器人上。

title: VLA-RAIL: A Real-Time Asynchronous Inference Linker for VLA Models and Robots
summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have achieved remarkable breakthroughs in robotics, with the action chunk playing a dominant role in these advances. Given the real-time and continuous nature of robotic motion control, the strategies for fusing a queue of successive action chunks have a profound impact on the overall performance of VLA models. Existing methods suffer from jitter, stalling, or even pauses in robotic action execution, which not only limits the achievable execution speed but also reduces the overall success rate of task completion. This paper introduces VLA-RAIL (A Real-Time Asynchronous Inference Linker), a novel framework designed to address these issues by conducting model inference and robot motion control asynchronously and guaranteeing smooth, continuous, and high-speed action execution. The core contributions of the paper are two fold: a Trajectory Smoother that effectively filters out the noise and jitter in the trajectory of one action chunk using polynomial fitting and a Chunk Fuser that seamlessly align the current executing trajectory and the newly arrived chunk, ensuring position, velocity, and acceleration continuity between two successive action chunks. We validate the effectiveness of VLA-RAIL on a benchmark of dynamic simulation tasks and several real-world manipulation tasks. Experimental results demonstrate that VLA-RAIL significantly reduces motion jitter, enhances execution speed, and improves task success rates, which will become a key infrastructure for the large-scale deployment of VLA models.
tldr: The paper introduces VLA-RAIL, a framework for asynchronous inference and motion control in VLA models, aimed at improving smoothness, speed, and success rates in robotic tasks using a trajectory smoother and chunk fuser.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种名为VLA-RAIL的框架，用于VLA模型中的异步推理和运动控制，旨在通过轨迹平滑器和块融合器提高机器人任务的平滑性、速度和成功率。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言-动作(VLA)模型在机器人技术领域取得了显著突破，其中动作块在这些进展中发挥了主导作用。鉴于机器人运动控制的实时性和连续性，融合一系列连续动作块的策略对VLA模型的整体性能产生深远影响。现有方法存在机器人动作执行过程中的抖动、停滞甚至暂停等问题，这不仅限制了可实现的执行速度，还降低了任务完成的整体成功率。本文提出VLA-RAIL（实时异步推理链接器），一种旨在解决这些问题的新型框架，通过异步地进行模型推理和机器人运动控制，保证平滑、连续和高速的动作执行。本文的核心贡献有两个方面：一是轨迹平滑器，它使用多项式拟合有效地过滤掉一个动作块轨迹中的噪声和抖动；二是动作块融合器，它无缝地对齐当前正在执行的轨迹和新到达的动作块，确保两个连续动作块之间的位置、速度和加速度的连续性。我们在动态仿真任务和几个真实世界的操作任务的基准上验证了VLA-RAIL的有效性。实验结果表明，VLA-RAIL显著减少运动抖动，提高执行速度，并提高任务成功率，这将成为VLA模型大规模部署的关键基础设施。

title: Hybrid Motion Planning with Deep Reinforcement Learning for Mobile Robot Navigation
summary: Autonomous mobile robots operating in complex, dynamic environments face the dual challenge of navigating large-scale, structurally diverse spaces with static obstacles while safely interacting with various moving agents. Traditional graph-based planners excel at long-range pathfinding but lack reactivity, while Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) methods demonstrate strong collision avoidance but often fail to reach distant goals due to a lack of global context. We propose Hybrid Motion Planning with Deep Reinforcement Learning (HMP-DRL), a hybrid framework that bridges this gap. Our approach utilizes a graph-based global planner to generate a path, which is integrated into a local DRL policy via a sequence of checkpoints encoded in both the state space and reward function. To ensure social compliance, the local planner employs an entity-aware reward structure that dynamically adjusts safety margins and penalties based on the semantic type of surrounding agents. We validate the proposed method through extensive testing in a realistic simulation environment derived from real-world map data. Comprehensive experiments demonstrate that HMP-DRL consistently outperforms other methods, including state-of-the-art approaches, in terms of key metrics of robot navigation: success rate, collision rate, and time to reach the goal. Overall, these findings confirm that integrating long-term path guidance with semantically-aware local control significantly enhances both the safety and reliability of autonomous navigation in complex human-centric settings.
tldr: The paper introduces HMP-DRL, a hybrid approach combining graph-based planning and DRL for robust mobile robot navigation in complex environments, demonstrating superior performance in simulation compared to other methods.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种混合方法HMP-DRL，结合了基于图的规划和深度强化学习，用于在复杂环境中实现鲁棒的移动机器人导航，并在仿真中展示了优于其他方法的性能。
summary_zh: 在复杂、动态环境中运行的自主移动机器人面临着双重挑战：在具有静态障碍物的大规模、结构多样的空间中导航，同时与各种移动Agent安全交互。传统的基于图的规划器擅长长程路径规划，但缺乏反应性；而深度强化学习（DRL）方法表现出强大的避障能力，但由于缺乏全局上下文，往往无法到达远处的目标。我们提出了一种混合运动规划与深度强化学习（HMP-DRL）方法，这是一种弥合这一差距的混合框架。我们的方法利用基于图的全局规划器生成一条路径，并通过一系列编码在状态空间和奖励函数中的检查点，将其集成到局部 DRL 策略中。为了确保社会合规性，局部规划器采用了一种实体感知奖励结构，该结构根据周围Agent的语义类型动态调整安全裕度和惩罚。我们通过在从真实世界地图数据中导出的逼真仿真环境中进行广泛测试来验证所提出的方法。综合实验表明，HMP-DRL 在机器人导航的关键指标（成功率、碰撞率和到达目标的时间）方面始终优于其他方法，包括最先进的方法。总而言之，这些发现证实了将长期路径引导与语义感知的局部控制相结合，能够显著提高在复杂以人为中心的环境中自主导航的安全性和可靠性。

title: Resolving State Ambiguity in Robot Manipulation via Adaptive Working Memory Recoding
summary: State ambiguity is common in robotic manipulation. Identical observations may correspond to multiple valid behavior trajectories. The visuomotor policy must correctly extract the appropriate types and levels of information from the history to identify the current task phase. However, naively extending the history window is computationally expensive and may cause severe overfitting. Inspired by the continuous nature of human reasoning and the recoding of working memory, we introduce PAM, a novel visuomotor Policy equipped with Adaptive working Memory. With minimal additional training cost in a two-stage manner, PAM supports a 300-frame history window while maintaining high inference speed. Specifically, a hierarchical frame feature extractor yields two distinct representations for motion primitives and temporal disambiguation. For compact representation, a context router with range-specific queries is employed to produce compact context features across multiple history lengths. And an auxiliary objective of reconstructing historical information is introduced to ensure that the context router acts as an effective bottleneck. We meticulously design 7 tasks and verify that PAM can handle multiple scenarios of state ambiguity simultaneously. With a history window of approximately 10 seconds, PAM still supports stable training and maintains inference speeds above 20Hz. Project website: https://tinda24.github.io/pam/
tldr: The paper introduces PAM, a novel visuomotor policy with Adaptive working Memory for robot manipulation that addresses state ambiguity by efficiently processing long history windows. It uses a hierarchical feature extractor and context router with an auxiliary reconstruction loss to achieve high inference speed with long context.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种新的视觉运动策略PAM，它具有自适应工作记忆，用于机器人操作，通过有效地处理长时间历史窗口来解决状态不明确的问题。 它使用分层特征提取器和上下文路由器以及辅助重构损失，从而在长上下文下实现高推理速度。
summary_zh: 状态歧义在机器人操控中很常见。相同的观测结果可能对应多个有效的行为轨迹。视觉运动策略必须正确地从历史信息中提取适当的类型和级别的信息，以识别当前的任务阶段。然而，简单地扩展历史窗口计算成本很高，并且可能导致严重的过拟合。受到人类推理的连续性和工作记忆的重编码的启发，我们提出了一种新颖的视觉运动策略PAM，它配备了自适应工作记忆。通过两阶段的最小附加训练成本，PAM支持300帧的历史窗口，同时保持较高的推理速度。具体来说，一个分层帧特征提取器生成了运动原语和时间消歧两种不同的表示。为了实现紧凑的表示，采用具有范围特定查询的上下文路由来生成跨多个历史长度的紧凑上下文特征。引入重构历史信息的辅助目标，以确保上下文路由作为有效的瓶颈。我们精心设计了7个任务，并验证了PAM可以同时处理多种状态歧义场景。在约10秒的历史窗口下，PAM仍然支持稳定的训练，并保持20Hz以上的推理速度。项目网站：https://tinda24.github.io/pam/

title: What Drives Success in Physical Planning with Joint-Embedding Predictive World Models?
summary: A long-standing challenge in AI is to develop agents capable of solving a wide range of physical tasks and generalizing to new, unseen tasks and environments. A popular recent approach involves training a world model from state-action trajectories and subsequently use it with a planning algorithm to solve new tasks. Planning is commonly performed in the input space, but a recent family of methods has introduced planning algorithms that optimize in the learned representation space of the world model, with the promise that abstracting irrelevant details yields more efficient planning. In this work, we characterize models from this family as JEPA-WMs and investigate the technical choices that make algorithms from this class work. We propose a comprehensive study of several key components with the objective of finding the optimal approach within the family. We conducted experiments using both simulated environments and real-world robotic data, and studied how the model architecture, the training objective, and the planning algorithm affect planning success. We combine our findings to propose a model that outperforms two established baselines, DINO-WM and V-JEPA-2-AC, in both navigation and manipulation tasks. Code, data and checkpoints are available at https://github.com/facebookresearch/jepa-wms.
tldr: This paper analyzes Joint-Embedding Predictive World Models (JEPA-WMs) for physical planning, identifying key components that contribute to their success and proposing a model that outperforms existing baselines in navigation and manipulation tasks.
tldr_zh: 本文分析了用于物理规划的联合嵌入预测世界模型(JEPA-WMs)， 识别了促成其成功的关键组件，并提出了一种模型，该模型在导航和操作任务中优于现有的基线。
summary_zh: 人工智能领域长期存在的挑战是开发能够解决各种物理任务并推广到新的、未见过的任务和环境中的智能体。一种流行的最新方法包括从状态-动作轨迹中训练世界模型，并随后将其与规划算法一起使用以解决新任务。规划通常在输入空间中进行，但最近一系列方法引入了在世界模型的学习表征空间中进行优化的规划算法，其承诺是抽象掉无关的细节可以提高规划效率。在这项工作中，我们将该系列模型归类为JEPA-WMs，并研究使此类算法起作用的技术选择。我们对几个关键组件进行了全面的研究，目标是找到该系列中的最佳方法。我们使用模拟环境和真实世界的机器人数据进行了实验，并研究了模型架构、训练目标和规划算法如何影响规划成功。我们将我们的发现结合起来，提出了一种模型，该模型在导航和操作任务中均优于两个已建立的基线，DINO-WM和V-JEPA-2-AC。代码、数据和检查点可在https://github.com/facebookresearch/jepa-wms上找到。

title: Foundation models on the bridge: Semantic hazard detection and safety maneuvers for maritime autonomy with vision-language models
summary: The draft IMO MASS Code requires autonomous and remotely supervised maritime vessels to detect departures from their operational design domain, enter a predefined fallback that notifies the operator, permit immediate human override, and avoid changing the voyage plan without approval. Meeting these obligations in the alert-to-takeover gap calls for a short-horizon, human-overridable fallback maneuver. Classical maritime autonomy stacks struggle when the correct action depends on meaning (e.g., diver-down flag means people in the water, fire close by means hazard). We argue (i) that vision-language models (VLMs) provide semantic awareness for such out-of-distribution situations, and (ii) that a fast-slow anomaly pipeline with a short-horizon, human-overridable fallback maneuver makes this practical in the handover window. We introduce Semantic Lookout, a camera-only, candidate-constrained vision-language model (VLM) fallback maneuver selector that selects one cautious action (or station-keeping) from water-valid, world-anchored trajectories under continuous human authority. On 40 harbor scenes we measure per-call scene understanding and latency, alignment with human consensus (model majority-of-three voting), short-horizon risk-relief on fire hazard scenes, and an on-water alert->fallback maneuver->operator handover. Sub-10 s models retain most of the awareness of slower state-of-the-art models. The fallback maneuver selector outperforms geometry-only baselines and increases standoff distance on fire scenes. A field run verifies end-to-end operation. These results support VLMs as semantic fallback maneuver selectors compatible with the draft IMO MASS Code, within practical latency budgets, and motivate future work on domain-adapted, hybrid autonomy that pairs foundation-model semantics with multi-sensor bird's-eye-view perception and short-horizon replanning.
tldr: This paper introduces Semantic Lookout, a VLM-based system for maritime autonomy that detects semantic hazards and triggers safe fallback maneuvers in accordance with IMO MASS Code, demonstrating its effectiveness in harbor scenes.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍Semantic Lookout，一个基于VLM的海事自主系统，能够检测语义危险并触发安全回退操作，符合IMO MASS规范，并在港口场景中展示了其有效性。
summary_zh: IMO MASS草案要求自主和远程监控的海事船舶能够检测到超出其运行设计域的偏差，进入预定义的后备状态并通知操作员，允许立即的人工干预，并且未经批准不得更改航行计划。在警报到接管的间隙中满足这些义务需要一个短时程、可人工干预的后备操作。当正确的行动取决于语义时（例如，潜水员下水旗意味着有人在水中，附近的火灾意味着危险），经典的海事自主系统难以应对。我们论证：（i）视觉-语言模型（VLM）为此类超出分布的情况提供语义感知，以及（ii）具有短时程、可人工干预的后备操作的快-慢异常管道使其在交接窗口中切实可行。我们引入了语义了望台（Semantic Lookout），这是一个仅使用摄像头、候选约束的视觉-语言模型（VLM）后备操作选择器，它在持续的人工授权下，从水中有效的、与世界锚定的轨迹中选择一个谨慎的行动（或保持船位）。在40个港口场景中，我们测量了每次调用的场景理解和延迟、与人类共识的一致性（模型三人表决），火灾危险场景的短时程风险缓解以及水上警报->后备操作->操作员交接。低于10秒的模型保留了最先进的较慢模型的大部分感知能力。后备操作选择器优于仅使用几何信息的基线，并增加了在火灾场景中的安全距离。现场运行验证了端到端操作。这些结果支持VLM作为语义后备操作选择器，其与IMO MASS草案兼容，在实际延迟预算范围内，并促进未来在领域自适应的混合自主方向的工作，该自主系统将基础模型语义与多传感器鸟瞰感知和短时程重规划相结合。

title: Subsecond 3D Mesh Generation for Robot Manipulation
summary: 3D meshes are a fundamental representation widely used in computer science and engineering. In robotics, they are particularly valuable because they capture objects in a form that aligns directly with how robots interact with the physical world, enabling core capabilities such as predicting stable grasps, detecting collisions, and simulating dynamics. Although automatic 3D mesh generation methods have shown promising progress in recent years, potentially offering a path toward real-time robot perception, two critical challenges remain. First, generating high-fidelity meshes is prohibitively slow for real-time use, often requiring tens of seconds per object. Second, mesh generation by itself is insufficient. In robotics, a mesh must be contextually grounded, i.e., correctly segmented from the scene and registered with the proper scale and pose. Additionally, unless these contextual grounding steps remain efficient, they simply introduce new bottlenecks. In this work, we introduce an end-to-end system that addresses these challenges, producing a high-quality, contextually grounded 3D mesh from a single RGB-D image in under one second. Our pipeline integrates open-vocabulary object segmentation, accelerated diffusion-based mesh generation, and robust point cloud registration, each optimized for both speed and accuracy. We demonstrate its effectiveness in a real-world manipulation task, showing that it enables meshes to be used as a practical, on-demand representation for robotics perception and planning.
tldr: This paper introduces an end-to-end system for generating high-quality, contextually grounded 3D meshes from single RGB-D images in under one second, addressing a critical bottleneck in real-time robot perception and manipulation.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一个端到端系统，该系统可以在一秒内从单个RGB-D图像生成高质量、上下文相关的3D网格，解决了实时机器人感知和操作中的关键瓶颈。
summary_zh: 三维网格是计算机科学和工程领域广泛应用的一种基本表示形式。在机器人技术中，它们尤其有价值，因为它们以一种与机器人和物理世界交互方式直接对齐的形式捕捉物体，从而实现诸如预测稳定抓取、检测碰撞和模拟动力学等核心功能。尽管近年来自动三维网格生成方法已经显示出令人鼓舞的进展，有望提供一种实现实时机器人感知的途径，但仍存在两个关键挑战。首先，生成高保真网格的速度非常慢，无法满足实时使用，通常每个对象需要花费数十秒。其次，仅靠网格生成是不够的。在机器人技术中，网格必须在上下文中进行定位，即从场景中正确分割并以正确的比例和姿态进行配准。此外，除非这些上下文定位步骤保持高效，否则它们只会引入新的瓶颈。在这项工作中，我们介绍了一种端到端系统，旨在解决这些挑战，从单个RGB-D图像中在不到一秒的时间内生成高质量、上下文定位的三维网格。我们的pipeline集成了开放词汇对象分割、加速的基于扩散的网格生成和鲁棒的点云配准，每一步都针对速度和准确性进行了优化。我们在一个真实世界的操作任务中展示了它的有效性，表明它使网格能够用作机器人感知和规划的一种实用、按需的表示形式。

title: Let It Flow: Agentic Crafting on Rock and Roll, Building the ROME Model within an Open Agentic Learning Ecosystem
summary: Agentic crafting requires LLMs to operate in real-world environments over multiple turns by taking actions, observing outcomes, and iteratively refining artifacts. Despite its importance, the open-source community lacks a principled, end-to-end ecosystem to streamline agent development. We introduce the Agentic Learning Ecosystem (ALE), a foundational infrastructure that optimizes the production pipeline for agent LLMs. ALE consists of three components: ROLL, a post-training framework for weight optimization; ROCK, a sandbox environment manager for trajectory generation; and iFlow CLI, an agent framework for efficient context engineering. We release ROME (ROME is Obviously an Agentic Model), an open-source agent grounded by ALE and trained on over one million trajectories. Our approach includes data composition protocols for synthesizing complex behaviors and a novel policy optimization algorithm, Interaction-based Policy Alignment (IPA), which assigns credit over semantic interaction chunks rather than individual tokens to improve long-horizon training stability. Empirically, we evaluate ROME within a structured setting and introduce Terminal Bench Pro, a benchmark with improved scale and contamination control. ROME demonstrates strong performance across benchmarks like SWE-bench Verified and Terminal Bench, proving the effectiveness of the ALE infrastructure.
tldr: The paper introduces the Agentic Learning Ecosystem (ALE), including ROLL, ROCK, and iFlow CLI, used to train ROME, an open-source agent. ROME demonstrates strong performance across various benchmarks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了Agentic Learning Ecosystem (ALE)，包含ROLL、ROCK和iFlow CLI，用于训练ROME，一个开源agent。ROME在多个基准测试中表现出色。
summary_zh: 代理性创作需要大型语言模型（LLM）在真实世界环境中通过多次交互，采取行动、观察结果并迭代改进制品。尽管其重要性，但开源社区缺乏一个有原则的、端到端的生态系统来简化代理的开发。我们引入了代理学习生态系统（ALE），这是一个优化代理 LLM 生产流程的基础设施。ALE 由三个组件组成：ROLL，一个用于权重优化的后训练框架；ROCK，一个用于轨迹生成的沙盒环境管理器；以及 iFlow CLI，一个用于高效上下文工程的代理框架。我们发布了 ROME（ROME 显然是一个代理模型），一个基于 ALE 构建并经过超百万轨迹训练的开源代理。我们的方法包括用于合成复杂行为的数据组合协议，以及一种新颖的策略优化算法，即基于交互的策略对齐（IPA），它将信用分配给语义交互块而不是单个token，以提高长程训练的稳定性。在经验上，我们在一个结构化环境中评估 ROME，并引入了规模和污染控制得到改善的 Terminal Bench Pro 基准测试。ROME 在 SWE-bench Verified 和 Terminal Bench 等基准测试中表现出强大的性能，证明了 ALE 基础设施的有效性。

title: Video and Language Alignment in 2D Systems for 3D Multi-object Scenes with Multi-Information Derivative-Free Control
summary: Cross-modal systems trained on 2D visual inputs are presented with a dimensional shift when processing 3D scenes. An in-scene camera bridges the dimensionality gap but requires learning a control module. We introduce a new method that improves multivariate mutual information estimates by regret minimisation with derivative-free optimisation. Our algorithm enables off-the-shelf cross-modal systems trained on 2D visual inputs to adapt online to object occlusions and differentiate features. The pairing of expressive measures and value-based optimisation assists control of an in-scene camera to learn directly from the noisy outputs of vision-language models. The resulting pipeline improves performance in cross-modal tasks on multi-object 3D scenes without resorting to pretraining or finetuning.
tldr: This paper introduces a method for adapting 2D-trained vision-language models to 3D multi-object scenes using an in-scene camera controlled by derivative-free optimization, improving performance without pre-training or fine-tuning.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种将2D训练的视觉-语言模型适配到3D多物体场景的方法，使用一个由无导数优化控制的场景内摄像头，无需预训练或微调即可提高性能。
summary_zh: 训练于2D视觉输入的跨模态系统在处理3D场景时面临维度转移。场景内相机可以弥合维度差距，但需要学习一个控制模块。我们提出了一种新方法，通过无导数优化的后悔值最小化来改进多元互信息估计。我们的算法使训练于2D视觉输入的现成跨模态系统能够在对象遮挡情况下在线适应并区分特征。表达性度量和基于价值的优化相结合，有助于控制场景内相机，直接从视觉-语言模型的噪声输出中学习。由此产生的流程提高了多对象3D场景上跨模态任务的性能，而无需诉诸预训练或微调。

title: Evolving, Not Training: Zero-Shot Reasoning Segmentation via Evolutionary Prompting
summary: Reasoning Segmentation requires models to interpret complex, context-dependent linguistic queries to achieve pixel-level localization. Current dominant approaches rely heavily on Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) or Reinforcement Learning (RL). However, SFT suffers from catastrophic forgetting and domain dependency, while RL is often hindered by training instability and rigid reliance on predefined reward functions. Although recent training-free methods circumvent these training burdens, they are fundamentally limited by a static inference paradigm. These methods typically rely on a single-pass "generate-then-segment" chain, which suffers from insufficient reasoning depth and lacks the capability to self-correct linguistic hallucinations or spatial misinterpretations. In this paper, we challenge these limitations and propose EVOL-SAM3, a novel zero-shot framework that reformulates reasoning segmentation as an inference-time evolutionary search process. Instead of relying on a fixed prompt, EVOL-SAM3 maintains a population of prompt hypotheses and iteratively refines them through a "Generate-Evaluate-Evolve" loop. We introduce a Visual Arena to assess prompt fitness via reference-free pairwise tournaments, and a Semantic Mutation operator to inject diversity and correct semantic errors. Furthermore, a Heterogeneous Arena module integrates geometric priors with semantic reasoning to ensure robust final selection. Extensive experiments demonstrate that EVOL-SAM3 not only substantially outperforms static baselines but also significantly surpasses fully supervised state-of-the-art methods on the challenging ReasonSeg benchmark in a zero-shot setting. The code is available at https://github.com/AHideoKuzeA/Evol-SAM3.
tldr: The paper introduces EVOL-SAM3, a zero-shot reasoning segmentation framework that uses an evolutionary search over prompts with a novel 'Generate-Evaluate-Evolve' loop, outperforming both static baselines and supervised methods on the ReasonSeg benchmark.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了EVOL-SAM3，一种零样本推理分割框架，它使用基于新型“生成-评估-进化”循环的提示进化搜索，在ReasonSeg基准测试中优于静态基线和监督方法。
summary_zh: 推理分割要求模型能够理解复杂的、上下文相关的语言查询，以实现像素级别的定位。当前的主流方法严重依赖于监督微调（SFT）或强化学习（RL）。然而，SFT 存在灾难性遗忘和领域依赖性；而 RL 常受困于训练不稳定和对预定义奖励函数的刚性依赖。尽管最近的免训练方法绕开了这些训练负担，但它们从根本上受到静态推理范式的限制。这些方法通常依赖于单次“生成然后分割”链，缺乏足够的推理深度，也无法自我纠正语言幻觉或空间误解。在本文中，我们挑战了这些限制，并提出EVOL-SAM3，一种新颖的零样本框架，它将推理分割重构为推理时的进化搜索过程。EVOL-SAM3 不是依赖于固定的提示，而是维护一个提示假设的种群，并通过“生成-评估-进化”循环迭代地改进它们。我们引入了一个视觉竞技场，通过无参考的成对比赛来评估提示的适应度，以及一个语义突变算子，注入多样性并纠正语义错误。此外，一个异构竞技场模块将几何先验与语义推理相结合，以确保稳健的最终选择。大量实验表明，EVOL-SAM3 不仅显著优于静态基线，还在具有挑战性的 ReasonSeg 基准测试中，以零样本的方式显著超越了全监督的最先进方法。代码可在https://github.com/AHideoKuzeA/Evol-SAM3 获取。

title: Youtu-Agent: Scaling Agent Productivity with Automated Generation and Hybrid Policy Optimization
summary: Existing Large Language Model (LLM) agent frameworks face two significant challenges: high configuration costs and static capabilities. Building a high-quality agent often requires extensive manual effort in tool integration and prompt engineering, while deployed agents struggle to adapt to dynamic environments without expensive fine-tuning. To address these issues, we propose \textbf{Youtu-Agent}, a modular framework designed for the automated generation and continuous evolution of LLM agents. Youtu-Agent features a structured configuration system that decouples execution environments, toolkits, and context management, enabling flexible reuse and automated synthesis. We introduce two generation paradigms: a \textbf{Workflow} mode for standard tasks and a \textbf{Meta-Agent} mode for complex, non-standard requirements, capable of automatically generating tool code, prompts, and configurations. Furthermore, Youtu-Agent establishes a hybrid policy optimization system: (1) an \textbf{Agent Practice} module that enables agents to accumulate experience and improve performance through in-context optimization without parameter updates; and (2) an \textbf{Agent RL} module that integrates with distributed training frameworks to enable scalable and stable reinforcement learning of any Youtu-Agents in an end-to-end, large-scale manner. Experiments demonstrate that Youtu-Agent achieves state-of-the-art performance on WebWalkerQA (71.47\%) and GAIA (72.8\%) using open-weight models. Our automated generation pipeline achieves over 81\% tool synthesis success rate, while the Practice module improves performance on AIME 2024/2025 by +2.7\% and +5.4\% respectively. Moreover, our Agent RL training achieves 40\% speedup with steady performance improvement on 7B LLMs, enhancing coding/reasoning and searching capabilities respectively up to 35\% and 21\% on Maths and general/multi-hop QA benchmarks.
tldr: The paper introduces Youtu-Agent, a modular framework for automated generation and continuous evolution of LLM agents, addressing configuration costs and static capabilities through automated tool synthesis, prompt engineering, and hybrid policy optimization, achieving state-of-the-art results on various benchmarks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了 Youtu-Agent，一个用于自动生成和持续演进LLM代理的模块化框架，通过自动化工具合成、提示工程和混合策略优化解决了配置成本高和静态能力的问题，并在各种基准测试中取得了最先进的结果。
summary_zh: 现有的大型语言模型（LLM）Agent框架面临两大挑战：高配置成本和静态能力。 通常，构建高质量的Agent需要在工具集成和提示工程方面投入大量的人工，而部署的Agent难以适应动态环境，并且需要昂贵的微调。为了解决这些问题，我们提出了\textbf{Youtu-Agent}，这是一个模块化框架，旨在实现LLM Agent的自动生成和持续进化。 Youtu-Agent具有结构化的配置系统，可以将执行环境、工具包和上下文管理解耦，从而实现灵活的重用和自动合成。我们引入了两种生成范式：用于标准任务的\textbf{Workflow}模式和用于复杂的、非标准需求的\textbf{Meta-Agent}模式，后者能够自动生成工具代码、提示和配置。此外，Youtu-Agent建立了一个混合策略优化系统：（1）一个\textbf{Agent Practice}模块，使Agent能够积累经验并通过上下文优化来提高性能，而无需参数更新；（2）一个\textbf{Agent RL}模块，该模块与分布式训练框架集成，以实现任何Youtu-Agent的端到端、大规模的可扩展且稳定的强化学习。实验表明，Youtu-Agent在使用开源模型时，在WebWalkerQA（71.47%）和GAIA（72.8%）上取得了最先进的性能。我们的自动化生成流程实现了超过81%的工具合成成功率，而Practice模块分别将AIME 2024/2025的性能提高了+2.7%和+5.4%。此外，我们的Agent RL训练实现了40%的加速，并在7B LLMs上实现了稳定的性能提升，在数学和通用/多跳问答基准测试中,分别将编码/推理和搜索能力提升高达35%和21%。

title: Reinforcement Learning-Augmented LLM Agents for Collaborative Decision Making and Performance Optimization
summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) perform well in language tasks but often lack collaborative awareness and struggle to optimize global performance in multi-agent settings. We present a reinforcement learning-augmented LLM agent framework that formulates cooperation as a decentralized partially observable Markov decision process (Dec-POMDP) and adopts centralized training with decentralized execution (CTDE). We introduce Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) to jointly optimize agent policies with access to global signals during training, together with a simplified joint reward that balances task quality, speed, and coordination cost. On collaborative writing and coding benchmarks, our framework delivers a 3x increase in task processing speed over single-agent baselines, 98.7% structural/style consistency in writing, and a 74.6% test pass rate in coding. The approach consistently outperforms strong multi-agent LLM baselines and provides a practical path toward reliable collaboration in complex workflows.
tldr: This paper presents a reinforcement learning-augmented LLM agent framework (GRPO) for collaborative tasks, achieving significant improvements in speed, consistency, and success rates on collaborative writing and coding benchmarks compared to existing methods.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种基于强化学习增强的LLM智能体框架(GRPO)，用于协同任务。相比现有方法，在协同写作和编码基准测试中，该框架在速度、一致性和成功率方面都取得了显著提升。
summary_zh: 大型语言模型 (LLMs) 在语言任务中表现出色，但通常缺乏协作意识，并且难以在多智能体环境中优化全局性能。我们提出了一种强化学习增强的 LLM 智能体框架，该框架将协作建模为去中心化部分可观测马尔可夫决策过程 (Dec-POMDP)，并采用集中式训练与去中心化执行 (CTDE)。我们引入了群体相对策略优化 (GRPO)，以便在训练期间通过访问全局信号来联合优化智能体策略，并采用简化的联合奖励来平衡任务质量、速度和协调成本。在协作写作和编码基准测试中，我们的框架能够以单智能体基准测试三倍的速度处理任务，在写作中实现 98.7% 的结构/风格一致性，并在编码中达到 74.6% 的测试通过率。该方法始终优于强大的多智能体 LLM 基线，并为复杂工作流程中的可靠协作提供了一条切实可行的途径。

title: From Building Blocks to Planning: Multi-Step Spatial Reasoning in LLMs with Reinforcement Learning
summary: Spatial reasoning in large language models (LLMs) has gained increasing attention due to applications in navigation and planning. Despite strong general language capabilities, LLMs still struggle with spatial transformations and multi-step planning in structured environments. We propose a two-stage approach that decomposes spatial reasoning into atomic building blocks and their composition. First, we apply supervised fine-tuning on elementary spatial transformations, such as rotation, translation, and scaling, to equip the model with basic spatial physics. We then freeze this physics-aware model and train lightweight LoRA adapters within the GRPO framework to learn policies that compose these building blocks for multi-step planning in puzzle-based environments, in a closed-loop manner. To support this pipeline, we synthesize an ASCII-art dataset and construct a corresponding ASCII-based reinforcement learning environment. Our method consistently outperforms baselines, including the generic backbone, physics-aware model, and end-to-end RL models, under both Dynamic environments with explicit state updates and Static environments where the model must rely on its internal state across steps. In addition, the proposed approach converges faster and exhibits more stable training compared to end-to-end reinforcement learning from scratch. Finally, we analyze attention patterns to assess whether fine-tuning induces meaningful improvements in spatial understanding.
tldr: The paper presents a two-stage approach using supervised fine-tuning for basic spatial transformations and reinforcement learning with LoRA adapters for multi-step planning in LLMs, showing improved performance and stability in puzzle-based environments.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一种两阶段方法，利用监督式微调进行基本的空间变换，并结合LoRA适配器的强化学习，用于LLM中的多步规划，在基于谜题的环境中表现出改进的性能和稳定性。
summary_zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）中的空间推理由于其在导航和规划领域的应用而受到越来越多的关注。尽管具有强大的通用语言能力，但 LLM 仍然难以应对结构化环境中的空间变换和多步规划。我们提出了一种两阶段方法，将空间推理分解为原子构建块及其组合。首先，我们对基本的空间变换（例如旋转、平移和缩放）应用监督式微调，使模型具备基本的空间物理知识。然后，我们冻结这个具有物理感知能力的模型，并在 GRPO 框架内训练轻量级的 LoRA 适配器，以学习策略，在基于谜题的环境中以闭环方式组合这些构建块，从而进行多步规划。为了支持这个流程，我们合成了一个 ASCII 艺术数据集，并构建了一个相应的基于 ASCII 的强化学习环境。我们的方法在动态环境（具有显式状态更新）和静态环境（模型必须依赖其内部状态跨步骤）下，始终优于包括通用骨干网络、具有物理感知能力的模型和端到端强化学习模型在内的基线模型。此外，与从头开始的端到端强化学习相比，所提出的方法收敛速度更快，并且表现出更稳定的训练过程。最后，我们分析注意力模式，以评估微调是否能诱导空间理解方面的有意义的改进。

title: Align While Search: Belief-Guided Exploratory Inference for World-Grounded Embodied Agents
summary: In this paper, we propose a test-time adaptive agent that performs exploratory inference through posterior-guided belief refinement without relying on gradient-based updates or additional training for LLM agent operating under partial observability. Our agent maintains an external structured belief over the environment state, iteratively updates it via action-conditioned observations, and selects actions by maximizing predicted information gain over the belief space. We estimate information gain using a lightweight LLM-based surrogate and assess world alignment through a novel reward that quantifies the consistency between posterior belief and ground-truth environment configuration. Experiments show that our method outperforms inference-time scaling baselines such as prompt-augmented or retrieval-enhanced LLMs, in aligning with latent world states with significantly lower integration overhead.
tldr: The paper introduces a test-time adaptive agent that explores and infers world states by updating a belief using action-conditioned observations and selecting actions to maximize expected information gain, demonstrating improved alignment with ground truth compared to prompt-augmented LLMs.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种测试时自适应智能体，它通过使用行动条件下的观察更新信念并选择动作来最大化预期信息增益，以此探索和推断世界状态，并展示了相比于提示增强的LLM，该智能体与真实环境的一致性有所提高。
summary_zh: 本文提出一种测试时自适应智能体，该智能体在部分可观测环境下运行的大语言模型智能体中，通过后验引导的信念细化执行探索性推理，无需依赖基于梯度的更新或额外的训练。我们的智能体维护一个关于环境状态的外部结构化信念，通过基于动作条件下的观测迭代更新该信念，并通过最大化信念空间上的预测信息增益来选择动作。我们使用轻量级基于大语言模型的代理来估计信息增益，并通过一种新的奖励评估世界校准，该奖励量化了后验信念与真实环境配置之间的一致性。实验表明，我们的方法在与潜在世界状态对齐方面，优于推理时缩放基线，例如提示增强或检索增强的大语言模型，且集成开销显著降低。

title: PhysTalk: Language-driven Real-time Physics in 3D Gaussian Scenes
summary: Realistic visual simulations are omnipresent, yet their creation requires computing time, rendering, and expert animation knowledge. Open-vocabulary visual effects generation from text inputs emerges as a promising solution that can unlock immense creative potential. However, current pipelines lack both physical realism and effective language interfaces, requiring slow offline optimization. In contrast, PhysTalk takes a 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) scene as input and translates arbitrary user prompts into real time, physics based, interactive 4D animations. A large language model (LLM) generates executable code that directly modifies 3DGS parameters through lightweight proxies and particle dynamics. Notably, PhysTalk is the first framework to couple 3DGS directly with a physics simulator without relying on time consuming mesh extraction. While remaining open vocabulary, this design enables interactive 3D Gaussian animation via collision aware, physics based manipulation of arbitrary, multi material objects. Finally, PhysTalk is train-free and computationally lightweight: this makes 4D animation broadly accessible and shifts these workflows from a "render and wait" paradigm toward an interactive dialogue with a modern, physics-informed pipeline.
tldr: PhysTalk introduces a novel framework for real-time, physics-based 4D animation using 3D Gaussian Splatting, enabling interactive manipulation of objects via language prompts without requiring mesh extraction or training.
tldr_zh: PhysTalk 引入了一种新颖的框架，使用 3D Gaussian Splatting 实现实时的、基于物理的 4D 动画，无需网格提取或训练，即可通过语言提示进行交互式对象操作。
summary_zh: 逼真的视觉模拟无处不在，但其创建需要计算时间、渲染和专业的动画知识。从文本输入生成开放词汇视觉特效正成为一种有前景的解决方案，它可以释放巨大的创造潜力。然而，当前的流程缺乏物理真实感和有效的语言界面，需要缓慢的离线优化。相比之下，PhysTalk 以 3D 高斯溅射 (3DGS) 场景作为输入，并将任意用户提示转换为实时的、基于物理的、交互式 4D 动画。大型语言模型 (LLM) 生成可执行代码，通过轻量级代理和粒子动力学直接修改 3DGS 参数。值得注意的是，PhysTalk 是第一个将 3DGS 直接与物理模拟器耦合，而无需依赖耗时的网格提取的框架。在保持开放词汇的同时，这种设计支持通过碰撞感知、基于物理的任意多材料对象操作来进行交互式 3D 高斯动画。最后，PhysTalk 是免训练且计算轻量级的：这使得 4D 动画得以广泛应用，并将这些工作流程从“渲染并等待”的模式转变为与现代、基于物理的流程进行交互式对话。

title: Improving Few-Shot Change Detection Visual Question Answering via Decision-Ambiguity-guided Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
summary: Change detection visual question answering (CDVQA) requires answering text queries by reasoning about semantic changes in bi-temporal remote sensing images. A straightforward approach is to boost CDVQA performance with generic vision-language models via supervised fine-tuning (SFT). Despite recent progress, we observe that a significant portion of failures do not stem from clearly incorrect predictions, but from decision ambiguity, where the model assigns similar confidence to the correct answer and strong distractors. To formalize this challenge, we define Decision-Ambiguous Samples (DAS) as instances with a small probability margin between the ground-truth answer and the most competitive alternative. We argue that explicitly optimizing DAS is crucial for improving the discriminability and robustness of CDVQA models. To this end, we propose DARFT, a Decision-Ambiguity-guided Reinforcement Fine-Tuning framework that first mines DAS using an SFT-trained reference policy and then applies group-relative policy optimization on the mined subset. By leveraging multi-sample decoding and intra-group relative advantages, DARFT suppresses strong distractors and sharpens decision boundaries without additional supervision. Extensive experiments demonstrate consistent gains over SFT baselines, particularly under few-shot settings.
tldr: This paper introduces a Decision-Ambiguity-guided Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (DARFT) framework to improve Change Detection Visual Question Answering (CDVQA) performance, especially in few-shot settings, by explicitly optimizing decision-ambiguous samples using reinforcement learning.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种决策歧义引导的强化微调（DARFT）框架，旨在通过使用强化学习显式优化决策模糊样本，从而提高变化检测视觉问答（CDVQA）的性能，尤其是在少样本设置下。
summary_zh: 变化检测视觉问答 (CDVQA) 需要通过推理双时相遥感图像中的语义变化来回答文本查询。一种直接的方法是通过监督式微调 (SFT) 利用通用视觉-语言模型来提升 CDVQA 性能。尽管取得了近期进展，我们观察到很大一部分失败并非源于明显的错误预测，而是源于决策模糊性，即模型给予正确答案和强干扰项相似的置信度。为了形式化这一挑战，我们将决策模糊样本 (DAS) 定义为真实答案与最具竞争力的备选项之间概率差较小的样本。我们认为，显式地优化 DAS 对于提高 CDVQA 模型的区分性和鲁棒性至关重要。为此，我们提出了 DARFT，一种决策模糊性引导的强化微调框架，该框架首先使用 SFT 训练的参考策略挖掘 DAS，然后在挖掘的子集上应用组相对策略优化。通过利用多样本解码和组内相对优势，DARFT 抑制了强干扰项并锐化了决策边界，而无需额外的监督。大量实验表明，DARFT 相对于 SFT 基线具有持续的增益，尤其是在少样本设置下。

title: Lifting Vision: Ground to Aerial Localization with Reasoning Guided Planning
summary: Multimodal intelligence development recently show strong progress in visual understanding and high level reasoning. Though, most reasoning system still reply on textual information as the main medium for inference. This limit their effectiveness in spatial tasks such as visual navigation and geo-localization. This work discuss about the potential scope of this field and eventually propose an idea visual reasoning paradigm Geo-Consistent Visual Planning, our introduced framework called Visual Reasoning for Localization, or ViReLoc, which performs planning and localization using only visual representations. The proposed framework learns spatial dependencies and geometric relations that text based reasoning often suffer to understand. By encoding step by step inference in the visual domain and optimizing with reinforcement based objectives, ViReLoc plans routes between two given ground images. The system also integrates contrastive learning and adaptive feature interaction to align cross view perspectives and reduce viewpoint differences. Experiments across diverse navigation and localization scenarios show consistent improvements in spatial reasoning accuracy and cross view retrieval performance. These results establish visual reasoning as a strong complementary approach for navigation and localization, and show that such tasks can be performed without real time global positioning system data, leading to more secure navigation solutions.
tldr: The paper introduces ViReLoc, a visual reasoning framework that performs ground-to-aerial localization and navigation using only visual representations, demonstrating improved spatial reasoning accuracy without relying on GPS data.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一个名为ViReLoc的视觉推理框架，该框架仅使用视觉表示执行地面到空中定位和导航，无需依赖GPS数据，并展示了改进的空间推理准确性。
summary_zh: 多模态智能发展近期在视觉理解和高层推理方面取得了显著进展。尽管如此，大多数推理系统仍然依赖于文本信息作为主要的推理媒介。这限制了它们在视觉导航和地理定位等空间任务中的有效性。本文探讨了该领域的潜在范围，并最终提出了一种称为“地理一致性视觉规划”的视觉推理范式。我们提出的框架名为“用于定位的视觉推理”（ViReLoc），仅使用视觉表征进行规划和定位。该框架学习了基于文本的推理常常难以理解的空间依赖关系和几何关系。通过在视觉领域中对逐步推理进行编码，并利用基于强化学习的目标进行优化，ViReLoc可在两个给定的地面图像之间规划路线。该系统还集成了对比学习和自适应特征交互，以对齐跨视角透视并减少视点差异。在各种导航和定位场景中进行的实验表明，在空间推理准确性和跨视角检索性能方面均有持续的改进。这些结果确立了视觉推理作为导航和定位的强大补充方法，并表明此类任务可以在没有实时全球定位系统数据的情况下执行，从而实现更安全的导航解决方案。

title: MSACL: Multi-Step Actor-Critic Learning with Lyapunov Certificates for Exponentially Stabilizing Control
summary: Achieving provable stability in model-free reinforcement learning (RL) remains a challenge, particularly in balancing exploration with rigorous safety. This article introduces MSACL, a framework that integrates exponential stability theory with maximum entropy RL through multi-step Lyapunov certificate learning. Unlike methods relying on complex reward engineering, MSACL utilizes off-policy multi-step data to learn Lyapunov certificates satisfying theoretical stability conditions. By introducing Exponential Stability Labels (ESL) and a $λ$-weighted aggregation mechanism, the framework effectively balances the bias-variance trade-off in multi-step learning. Policy optimization is guided by a stability-aware advantage function, ensuring the learned policy promotes rapid Lyapunov descent. We evaluate MSACL across six benchmarks, including stabilization and nonlinear tracking tasks, demonstrating its superiority over state-of-the-art Lyapunov-based RL algorithms. MSACL achieves exponential stability and rapid convergence under simple rewards, while exhibiting significant robustness to uncertainties and generalization to unseen trajectories. Sensitivity analysis establishes the multi-step horizon $n=20$ as a robust default across diverse systems. By linking Lyapunov theory with off-policy actor-critic frameworks, MSACL provides a foundation for verifiably safe learning-based control. Source code and benchmark environments will be made publicly available.
tldr: The paper introduces MSACL, a multi-step actor-critic reinforcement learning framework that integrates Lyapunov stability theory for provably stable control, demonstrating superior performance in stabilization and tracking tasks compared to existing Lyapunov-based RL methods, with robustness to uncertainties and good generalization.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了MSACL，一个多步actor-critic强化学习框架，它结合了Lyapunov稳定性理论，用于可证明的稳定控制。与现有的基于Lyapunov的强化学习方法相比，MSACL在稳定和跟踪任务中表现出卓越的性能，对不确定性具有鲁棒性，并且具有良好的泛化能力。
summary_zh: 在免模型强化学习 (RL) 中实现可证明的稳定性仍然是一个挑战，尤其是在探索与严格安全性之间取得平衡。本文介绍了一种框架 MSACL，它通过多步 Lyapunov 证书学习将指数稳定性理论与最大熵 RL 相结合。与依赖复杂奖励工程的方法不同，MSACL 利用离线多步数据来学习满足理论稳定性条件的 Lyapunov 证书。通过引入指数稳定性标签 (ESL) 和 $λ$-加权聚合机制，该框架有效地平衡了多步学习中的偏差-方差权衡。策略优化由稳定性感知优势函数引导，确保学习到的策略促进快速 Lyapunov 下降。我们在包括稳定化和非线性跟踪任务在内的六个基准上评估了 MSACL，证明了其优于最先进的基于 Lyapunov 的 RL 算法。 MSACL 在简单的奖励下实现了指数稳定性和快速收敛，同时对不确定性表现出显著的鲁棒性，并能泛化到未见过的轨迹。敏感性分析确定了多步范围 $n=20$ 是跨不同系统的稳健默认值。通过将 Lyapunov 理论与离线式 Actor-Critic 框架联系起来，MSACL 为可验证的安全学习控制奠定了基础。源代码和基准环境将被公开提供。

title: Explaining Why Things Go Where They Go: Interpretable Constructs of Human Organizational Preferences
summary: Robotic systems for household object rearrangement often rely on latent preference models inferred from human demonstrations. While effective at prediction, these models offer limited insight into the interpretable factors that guide human decisions. We introduce an explicit formulation of object arrangement preferences along four interpretable constructs: spatial practicality (putting items where they naturally fit best in the space), habitual convenience (making frequently used items easy to reach), semantic coherence (placing items together if they are used for the same task or are contextually related), and commonsense appropriateness (putting things where people would usually expect to find them). To capture these constructs, we designed and validated a self-report questionnaire through a 63-participant online study. Results confirm the psychological distinctiveness of these constructs and their explanatory power across two scenarios (kitchen and living room). We demonstrate the utility of these constructs by integrating them into a Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) planner and show that when guided by participant-derived preferences, our planner can generate reasonable arrangements that closely align with those generated by participants. This work contributes a compact, interpretable formulation of object arrangement preferences and a demonstration of how it can be operationalized for robot planning.
tldr: This paper introduces a framework for understanding human object arrangement preferences based on four interpretable constructs, demonstrating its utility in guiding robot planning for household object rearrangement.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一个理解人类物体摆放偏好的框架，该框架基于四个可解释的因素，并展示了其在指导机器人进行家庭物体重新摆放规划方面的效用。
summary_zh: 用于家庭物品重排列的机器人系统通常依赖于从人类演示中推断出的潜在偏好模型。虽然这些模型在预测方面有效，但它们对指导人类决策的可解释因素提供的洞察力有限。我们引入了一种物体排列偏好的显式公式，该公式基于四个可解释的构建体：空间实用性（将物品放置在空间中最适合的位置）、习惯便利性（使常用物品易于取用）、语义一致性（如果物品用于同一任务或在上下文中相关，则将它们放置在一起）以及常识适当性（将物品放置在人们通常期望找到它们的地方）。为了捕捉这些构建体，我们通过一项有63名参与者的在线研究设计并验证了一份自我报告问卷。结果证实了这些构建体在心理上的独特性以及它们在两个场景（厨房和客厅）中的解释能力。我们通过将这些构建体整合到蒙特卡洛树搜索（MCTS）规划器中，并证明在参与者得出的偏好指导下，我们的规划器能够生成与参与者生成的排列高度一致的合理排列，从而展示了这些构建体的效用。这项工作贡献了一种紧凑、可解释的物体排列偏好公式，并展示了如何将其用于机器人规划。

title: Control of Microrobots with Reinforcement Learning under On-Device Compute Constraints
summary: An important function of autonomous microrobots is the ability to perform robust movement over terrain. This paper explores an edge ML approach to microrobot locomotion, allowing for on-device, lower latency control under compute, memory, and power constraints. This paper explores the locomotion of a sub-centimeter quadrupedal microrobot via reinforcement learning (RL) and deploys the resulting controller on an ultra-small system-on-chip (SoC), SC$μ$M-3C, featuring an ARM Cortex-M0 microcontroller running at 5 MHz. We train a compact FP32 multilayer perceptron (MLP) policy with two hidden layers ($[128, 64]$) in a massively parallel GPU simulation and enhance robustness by utilizing domain randomization over simulation parameters. We then study integer (Int8) quantization (per-tensor and per-feature) to allow for higher inference update rates on our resource-limited hardware, and we connect hardware power budgets to achievable update frequency via a cycles-per-update model for inference on our Cortex-M0. We propose a resource-aware gait scheduling viewpoint: given a device power budget, we can select the gait mode (trot/intermediate/gallop) that maximizes expected RL reward at a corresponding feasible update frequency. Finally, we deploy our MLP policy on a real-world large-scale robot on uneven terrain, qualitatively noting that domain-randomized training can improve out-of-distribution stability. We do not claim real-world large-robot empirical zero-shot transfer in this work.
tldr: This paper explores using reinforcement learning for microrobot locomotion with on-device computation constraints. It focuses on deploying a compact policy on a low-power microcontroller and optimizing gait scheduling based on power budgets.
tldr_zh: 本文探索了在设备计算限制下使用强化学习来进行微型机器人运动控制。重点在于将紧凑的策略部署在低功耗微控制器上，并根据功率预算优化步态调度。
summary_zh: 自主微型机器人的一个重要功能是在地形上进行鲁棒的运动。本文探讨了一种边缘机器学习方法用于微型机器人运动，从而在计算、内存和功率约束下实现设备上的、更低延迟的控制。本文研究了亚厘米级四足微型机器人通过强化学习 (RL) 实现的运动，并将生成的控制器部署在超小型片上系统 (SoC) SC$μ$M-3C 上，该系统配备了一个运行频率为 5 MHz 的 ARM Cortex-M0 微控制器。我们在大规模并行 GPU 仿真中训练了一个紧凑的 FP32 多层感知机 (MLP) 策略，该策略具有两个隐藏层 ($[128, 64]$)，并通过对仿真参数进行域随机化来增强鲁棒性。然后，我们研究了整数 (Int8) 量化（按张量和按特征），以便在资源受限的硬件上实现更高的推理更新率，并通过 Cortex-M0 推理的每次更新周期模型将硬件功耗预算与可实现的更新频率联系起来。我们提出了一种资源感知的步态调度视角：给定设备功耗预算，我们可以选择以相应的可行更新频率最大化预期 RL 奖励的步态模式（小跑/中间/疾驰）。最后，我们将我们的 MLP 策略部署在真实世界的不平坦地形上的大型机器人上，定性地注意到域随机化训练可以提高分布外稳定性。在这项工作中，我们不声称真实世界大型机器人能够进行经验性的零样本迁移。

title: ReSPIRe: Informative and Reusable Belief Tree Search for Robot Probabilistic Search and Tracking in Unknown Environments
summary: Target search and tracking (SAT) is a fundamental problem for various robotic applications such as search and rescue and environmental exploration. This paper proposes an informative trajectory planning approach, namely ReSPIRe, for SAT in unknown cluttered environments under considerably inaccurate prior target information and limited sensing field of view. We first develop a novel sigma point-based approximation approach to fast and accurately estimate mutual information reward under non-Gaussian belief distributions, utilizing informative sampling in state and observation spaces to mitigate the computational intractability of integral calculation. To tackle significant uncertainty associated with inadequate prior target information, we propose the hierarchical particle structure in ReSPIRe, which not only extracts critical particles for global route guidance, but also adjusts the particle number adaptively for planning efficiency. Building upon the hierarchical structure, we develop the reusable belief tree search approach to build a policy tree for online trajectory planning under uncertainty, which reuses rollout evaluation to improve planning efficiency. Extensive simulations and real-world experiments demonstrate that ReSPIRe outperforms representative benchmark methods with smaller MI approximation error, higher search efficiency, and more stable tracking performance, while maintaining outstanding computational efficiency.
tldr: The paper presents ReSPIRe, a novel informative trajectory planning approach for target search and tracking in unknown environments, utilizing a hierarchical particle structure and reusable belief tree search to improve search efficiency and tracking performance.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了ReSPIRe，一种用于在未知环境中进行目标搜索和跟踪的新型信息轨迹规划方法，该方法利用分层粒子结构和可重用置信树搜索来提高搜索效率和跟踪性能。
summary_zh: 目标搜索与跟踪（SAT）是各种机器人应用（如搜索救援和环境探索）中的一个基本问题。本文提出了一种信息丰富的轨迹规划方法，即ReSPIRe，用于在未知的杂乱环境中，在相当不准确的先验目标信息和有限的传感视野下进行SAT。我们首先开发了一种基于Sigma点的近似方法，以快速准确地估计非高斯置信度分布下的互信息奖励，并利用状态空间和观测空间中的信息采样来缓解积分计算的计算复杂性。为了解决与不足的先验目标信息相关的显著不确定性，我们在ReSPIRe中提出了分层粒子结构，该结构不仅提取关键粒子以用于全局路线指导，而且还会自适应地调整粒子数量以提高规划效率。在此分层结构的基础上，我们开发了可重用的置信度树搜索方法，以构建用于不确定性下在线轨迹规划的策略树，该方法通过重用rollout评估来提高规划效率。大量的仿真和真实世界实验表明，ReSPIRe在更小的互信息近似误差、更高的搜索效率和更稳定的跟踪性能方面优于具有代表性的基准方法，同时保持了出色的计算效率。

title: Antagonistic Bowden-Cable Actuation of a Lightweight Robotic Hand: Toward Dexterous Manipulation for Payload Constrained Humanoids
summary: Humanoid robots toward human-level dexterity require robotic hands capable of simultaneously providing high grasping force, rapid actuation speeds, multiple degrees of freedom, and lightweight structures within human-like size constraints. Meeting these conflicting requirements remains challenging, as satisfying this combination typically necessitates heavier actuators and bulkier transmission systems, significantly restricting the payload capacity of robot arms. In this letter, we present a lightweight anthropomorphic hand actuated by Bowden cables, which uniquely combines rolling-contact joint optimization with antagonistic cable actuation, enabling single-motor-per-joint control with negligible cable-length deviation. By relocating the actuator module to the torso, the design substantially reduces distal mass while maintaining anthropomorphic scale and dexterity. Additionally, this antagonistic cable actuation eliminates the need for synchronization between motors. Using the proposed methods, the hand assembly with a distal mass of 236g (excluding remote actuators and Bowden sheaths) demonstrated reliable execution of dexterous tasks, exceeding 18N fingertip force and lifting payloads over one hundred times its own mass. Furthermore, robustness was validated through Cutkosky taxonomy grasps and trajectory consistency under perturbed actuator-hand transformations.
tldr: This paper presents a lightweight anthropomorphic hand actuated by Bowden cables, achieving high grasping force and dexterity by relocating actuators and using antagonistic cable actuation, significantly reducing distal mass and simplifying control.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种由Bowden拉索驱动的轻型拟人化手，通过重新定位执行器和使用对抗性拉索驱动，实现了高的抓握力和灵活性，显著减少了末端质量并简化了控制。
summary_zh: 为了实现人类水平的灵巧性，人形机器人需要具备以下能力的机械手：在类似人类的尺寸约束下，能够同时提供高抓握力、快速驱动速度、多个自由度和轻量化结构。满足这些相互冲突的要求仍然具有挑战性，因为满足这种组合通常需要更重的执行器和更庞大的传动系统，从而显著限制了机器人手臂的有效载荷能力。在这封信中，我们提出了一种由鲍登电缆驱动的轻量化拟人机械手，它独特地将滚动接触关节优化与拮抗式电缆驱动相结合，从而能够实现每关节单电机控制，且电缆长度偏差可忽略不计。通过将执行器模块重新定位到躯干，该设计在保持拟人尺度和灵巧性的同时，显著降低了远端质量。此外，这种拮抗式电缆驱动消除了电机之间同步的需要。使用所提出的方法，该机械手组件（不包括远程执行器和鲍登护套），远端质量为236克，证明了灵巧任务的可靠执行，超过18N的指尖力，并能够举起超过其自身质量一百倍的有效载荷。此外，通过 Cutkosky 分类抓取和受到扰动的执行器-机械手变换下的轨迹一致性，验证了其鲁棒性。

title: DISF: Disentangled Iterative Surface Fitting for Contact-stable Grasp Planning with Grasp Pose Alignment to the Object Center of Mass
summary: In this work, we address the limitation of surface fitting-based grasp planning algorithm, which primarily focuses on geometric alignment between the gripper and object surface while overlooking the stability of contact point distribution, often resulting in unstable grasps due to inadequate contact configurations. To overcome this limitation, we propose a novel surface fitting algorithm that integrates contact stability while preserving geometric compatibility. Inspired by human grasping behavior, our method disentangles the grasp pose optimization into three sequential steps: (1) rotation optimization to align contact normals, (2) translation refinement to improve the alignment between the gripper frame origin and the object Center of Mass (CoM), and (3) gripper aperture adjustment to optimize contact point distribution. We validate our approach in simulation across 15 objects under both Known-shape (with clean CAD-derived dataset) and Observed-shape (with YCB object dataset) settings, including cross-platform grasp execution on three robot--gripper platforms. We further validate the method in real-world grasp experiments on a UR3e robot. Overall, DISF reduces CoM misalignment while maintaining geometric compatibility, translating into higher grasp success in both simulation and real-world execution compared to baselines. Additional videos and supplementary results are available on our project page: https://tomoya-yamanokuchi.github.io/disf-ras-project-page/
tldr: The paper introduces DISF, a novel surface fitting algorithm for grasp planning that integrates contact stability and geometric compatibility by disentangling grasp pose optimization, resulting in improved grasp success rates in simulation and the real world.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种新的表面拟合算法DISF，用于抓取规划，通过解耦抓取姿态优化，集成了接触稳定性和几何兼容性，从而提高了在模拟和现实世界中的抓取成功率。
summary_zh: 这项研究致力于解决基于表面拟合的抓取规划算法的局限性，此类算法主要关注夹爪与物体表面的几何对齐，而忽略了接触点分布的稳定性，常常由于不充分的接触配置而导致不稳定的抓取。 为了克服这一局限，我们提出了一种新颖的表面拟合算法，该算法在保持几何兼容性的同时，集成了接触稳定性。受到人类抓取行为的启发，我们的方法将抓取姿态优化分解为三个连续的步骤：（1）旋转优化，以对齐接触法向量；（2）平移优化，以改善夹爪坐标系原点与物体质心(CoM)之间的对齐；（3）夹爪开口调整，以优化接触点分布。我们通过在模拟环境中对15个物体进行验证，涵盖了已知形状（使用干净的CAD衍生数据集）和观测形状（使用YCB物体数据集）两种设置，并包括在三个机器人--夹爪平台上的跨平台抓取执行。我们还通过在UR3e机器人上的真实世界抓取实验验证了该方法。总体而言，DISF在保持几何兼容性的同时，降低了质心（CoM）的错位，从而在模拟和真实世界执行中都比基线方法实现了更高的抓取成功率。更多的视频和补充结果可以在我们的项目页面上找到：https://tomoya-yamanokuchi.github.io/disf-ras-project-page/

title: ShowUI-$π$: Flow-based Generative Models as GUI Dexterous Hands
summary: Building intelligent agents capable of dexterous manipulation is essential for achieving human-like automation in both robotics and digital environments. However, existing GUI agents rely on discrete click predictions (x,y), which prohibits free-form, closed-loop trajectories (e.g. dragging a progress bar) that require continuous, on-the-fly perception and adjustment. In this work, we develop ShowUI-$π$, the first flow-based generative model as GUI dexterous hand, featuring the following designs: (i) Unified Discrete-Continuous Actions, integrating discrete clicks and continuous drags within a shared model, enabling flexible adaptation across diverse interaction modes; (ii) Flow-based Action Generation for drag modeling, which predicts incremental cursor adjustments from continuous visual observations via a lightweight action expert, ensuring smooth and stable trajectories; (iii) Drag Training data and Benchmark, where we manually collect and synthesize 20K drag trajectories across five domains (e.g. PowerPoint, Adobe Premiere Pro), and introduce ScreenDrag, a benchmark with comprehensive online and offline evaluation protocols for assessing GUI agents' drag capabilities. Our experiments show that proprietary GUI agents still struggle on ScreenDrag (e.g. Operator scores 13.27, and the best Gemini-2.5-CUA reaches 22.18). In contrast, ShowUI-$π$ achieves 26.98 with only 450M parameters, underscoring both the difficulty of the task and the effectiveness of our approach. We hope this work advances GUI agents toward human-like dexterous control in digital world. The code is available at https://github.com/showlab/showui-pi.
tldr: The paper introduces ShowUI-π, a flow-based generative model for GUI agents, enabling both discrete clicks and continuous drags. It demonstrates superior performance compared to existing agents on a newly introduced ScreenDrag benchmark.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了ShowUI-π，一种基于流的生成模型，用于GUI代理，能够进行离散点击和连续拖动。它在新引入的ScreenDrag基准测试中表现优于现有代理。
summary_zh: 构建具备灵巧操作能力的智能体对于在机器人和数字环境中实现类人自动化至关重要。然而，现有的GUI智能体依赖于离散点击预测(x,y)，这限制了自由形式的闭环轨迹（例如，拖动进度条），而后者需要连续、实时的感知和调整。在这项工作中，我们开发了ShowUI-$π$，这是第一个基于流的生成模型，作为GUI灵巧手，具有以下设计：（i）统一的离散-连续动作，在共享模型中集成离散点击和连续拖动，从而实现跨各种交互模式的灵活适应；（ii）用于拖动建模的基于流的动作生成，通过轻量级的动作专家从连续视觉观测中预测增量光标调整，从而确保平滑稳定的轨迹；（iii）拖动训练数据和基准，我们手动收集并合成了跨越五个领域的2万条拖动轨迹（例如，PowerPoint、Adobe Premiere Pro），并引入了ScreenDrag，这是一个具有综合在线和离线评估协议的基准，用于评估GUI智能体的拖动能力。我们的实验表明，专有的GUI智能体在ScreenDrag上仍然表现不佳（例如，Operator得分13.27，而最佳的Gemini-2.5-CUA达到22.18）。相比之下，ShowUI-$π$仅使用4.5亿个参数就达到了26.98，突出了任务的难度以及我们方法的有效性。我们希望这项工作能够推动GUI智能体朝着在数字世界中实现类人灵巧控制的方向发展。代码可在https://github.com/showlab/showui-pi上找到。

title: AMAP Agentic Planning Technical Report
summary: We present STAgent, an agentic large language model tailored for spatio-temporal understanding, designed to solve complex tasks such as constrained point-of-interest discovery and itinerary planning. STAgent is a specialized model capable of interacting with ten distinct tools within spatio-temporal scenarios, enabling it to explore, verify, and refine intermediate steps during complex reasoning. Notably, STAgent effectively preserves its general capabilities. We empower STAgent with these capabilities through three key contributions: (1) a stable tool environment that supports over ten domain-specific tools, enabling asynchronous rollout and training; (2) a hierarchical data curation framework that identifies high-quality data like a needle in a haystack, curating high-quality queries with a filter ratio of 1:10,000, emphasizing both diversity and difficulty; and (3) a cascaded training recipe that starts with a seed SFT stage acting as a guardian to measure query difficulty, followed by a second SFT stage fine-tuned on queries with high certainty, and an ultimate RL stage that leverages data of low certainty. Initialized with Qwen3-30B-A3B to establish a strong SFT foundation and leverage insights into sample difficulty, STAgent yields promising performance on TravelBench while maintaining its general capabilities across a wide range of general benchmarks, thereby demonstrating the effectiveness of our proposed agentic model.
tldr: This paper presents STAgent, a large language model specialized for spatio-temporal understanding and itinerary planning, trained with a novel data curation and cascaded training approach, and demonstrates strong performance on TravelBench while preserving general capabilities.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了STAgent，一种专门用于时空理解和行程规划的大型语言模型，通过一种新的数据整理和级联训练方法进行训练，并在TravelBench上表现出强大的性能，同时保持了一般能力。
summary_zh: 我们提出了 STAgent，一个专为时空理解量身定制的 Agent 化大型语言模型，旨在解决诸如约束兴趣点发现和行程规划等复杂任务。STAgent 是一种专门的模型，能够在时空场景中与十种不同的工具交互，使其能够在复杂推理过程中探索、验证和改进中间步骤。值得注意的是，STAgent 有效地保留了其通用能力。我们通过三个关键贡献赋予 STAgent 这些能力：（1）一个稳定的工具环境，支持十多种特定领域工具，从而实现异步部署和训练；（2）一个分层数据管理框架，能够像大海捞针一样识别高质量数据，以 1:10,000 的过滤比例管理高质量查询，强调多样性和难度；（3）一个级联训练方案，首先以一个种子 SFT 阶段作为守卫来衡量查询难度，然后进行第二阶段 SFT，在具有高置信度的查询上进行微调，最后是一个利用低置信度数据的 RL 阶段。STAgent 以 Qwen3-30B-A3B 进行初始化，以建立强大的 SFT 基础并利用对样本难度的洞察，在 TravelBench 上取得了有希望的性能，同时在广泛的通用基准测试中保持了其通用能力，从而证明了我们提出的 Agent 化模型的有效性。

title: Thinking on Maps: How Foundation Model Agents Explore, Remember, and Reason Map Environments
summary: Map environments provide a fundamental medium for representing spatial structure. Understanding how foundation model (FM) agents understand and act in such environments is therefore critical for enabling reliable map-based reasoning and applications. However, most existing evaluations of spatial ability in FMs rely on static map inputs or text-based queries, overlooking the interactive and experience-driven nature of spatial understanding.In this paper, we propose an interactive evaluation framework to analyze how FM agents explore, remember, and reason in symbolic map environments. Agents incrementally explore partially observable grid-based maps consisting of roads, intersections, and points of interest (POIs), receiving only local observations at each step. Spatial understanding is then evaluated using six kinds of spatial tasks. By systematically varying exploration strategies, memory representations, and reasoning schemes across multiple foundation models, we reveal distinct functional roles of these components. Exploration primarily affects experience acquisition but has a limited impact on final reasoning accuracy. In contrast, memory representation plays a central role in consolidating spatial experience, with structured memories particularly sequential and graph-based representations, substantially improving performance on structure-intensive tasks such as path planning. Reasoning schemes further shape how stored spatial knowledge is used, with advanced prompts supporting more effective multi-step inference. We further observe that spatial reasoning performance saturates across model versions and scales beyond a certain capability threshold, indicating that improvements in map-based spatial understanding require mechanisms tailored to spatial representation and reasoning rather than scaling alone.
tldr: This paper introduces an interactive evaluation framework to study how foundation model agents explore, remember, and reason in symbolic map environments, highlighting the importance of memory representation and reasoning schemes over exploration strategies and model scaling.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一个交互式评估框架，用于研究基础模型智能体如何在符号地图环境中探索、记忆和推理，强调了记忆表示和推理方案相对于探索策略和模型扩展的重要性。
summary_zh:
地图环境为表示空间结构提供了一个基本的媒介。因此，理解基础模型（FM）智能体如何理解并在这种环境中行动，对于实现可靠的基于地图的推理和应用至关重要。然而，目前对 FM 空间能力的大多数评估依赖于静态地图输入或基于文本的查询，忽略了空间理解的互动性和经验驱动的本质。

在本文中，我们提出了一个交互式评估框架，用于分析 FM 智能体如何在符号地图环境中探索、记忆和推理。智能体以增量方式探索部分可观察的基于网格的地图，这些地图由道路、交叉路口和兴趣点（POI）组成，在每一步仅接收局部观测。然后使用六种空间任务来评估空间理解。通过系统地改变多个基础模型的探索策略、记忆表示和推理方案，我们揭示了这些组件的不同功能作用。探索主要影响经验获取，但对最终推理准确性的影响有限。相比之下，记忆表示在巩固空间经验方面起着核心作用，特别是结构化的记忆表示，如顺序和基于图的表示，可以显著提高路径规划等结构密集型任务的性能。推理方案进一步影响存储的空间知识的使用方式，高级提示支持更有效的多步推理。我们进一步观察到，空间推理性能在超过一定的能力阈值后，会随着模型版本和规模的增加而饱和，这表明基于地图的空间理解的改进需要针对空间表示和推理量身定制的机制，而不是单纯的规模扩大。

title: FoundationSLAM: Unleashing the Power of Depth Foundation Models for End-to-End Dense Visual SLAM
summary: We present FoundationSLAM, a learning-based monocular dense SLAM system that addresses the absence of geometric consistency in previous flow-based approaches for accurate and robust tracking and mapping.
